programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: Five months after the Polesford case, a heavily pregnant Helen is still battling with the fallout from her infidelity. Her life is derailed by a gangland shooting with tragic consequences. Just days from giving birth, she is plunged into a murder investigation involving Manchester's criminal underbelly, forcing her to question everything she holds dear. Drama, starring MyAnna Buring and Tim McInnerny

Medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd and Christabel Fellowes

programme.desc: The staff of Grace Brothers stage a life-size Punch and Judy show to entertain a group of their colleagues' children. Christmas special of the vintage comedy, starring John Inman

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Meanwhile, Sarah Caulfield has orders to murder Lucas, but struggles with her emotions, having fallen in love with him. Spy drama, starring Richard Armitage and Genevieve O'Reilly

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the third Sunday of Advent, Father John Dickson, Catholic Chaplain, leads the ecumenical service from the Chapel of Royal Holloway, University of London. With hymns and carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a new recording from the University's Chapel Choir

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm as the hunt heads to Lincoln, with Charlie Ross and Richard Madley helping the teams, as Eric learns about one of Lincoln's most famous sons

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Thursford in Norfolk to experience a Christmas light show and walks through the four-acre lantern extravaganza. Katie Piper visits a church in North London where their Christmas Angel hampers bring some festive cheer to families in need. Featuring favourite Christmas carols with performances from Collabro, Marisha Wallace, and opera star Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple in their search for their perfect retirement retreat in the Cornish countryside on a budget of £400,000. Jules also visits the National Lobster Hatchery to lend a hand in their conservation work

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Ellesmere Port, where Rachael, Lyndsey and daughter Missy-Mae want a garden to match their fun-loving personalities

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore puts in some hard graft at the nation's tips to uncover four items that can be transformed. Master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, upholsterer Anthony Devine and design guru Jay Blades are on hand to help reinvent the pieces of furniture into desirable pieces. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The semi-final results show opens with a routine from the Strictly professionals, and there's also a musical performance from pop superstars Little Mix. More importantly, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman also reveal which two couples will compete in the dance off, leaving the judges to decide who will not be making it through to the final

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra and Will find allies who can help them in their search for Will's father, the Magisterium learn something shocking, and Mrs Coulter meets a formidable foe. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: When 12-year-old Kingsley is transferred to a special-needs school, a group of West Indian women uncover an unofficial segregation policy preventing many black children from receiving the education they deserve. Co-writer/director Steve McQueen's drama, starring Kenyah Sandy and Sharlene Whyte

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman and guests review the day's Premier League action, featuring Fulham v Liverpool at Craven Cottage, Crystal Palace v Tottenham Hotspur at Selhurst Park, Arsenal v Burnley at Emirates Stadium, Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at King Power Stadium and Southampton v Sheffield United at St Mary's Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester City v Arsenal, Birmingham City v Everton and Reading v Manchester United

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties in Sutton in south London, Chatham in Kent and Middlesbrough, and talk to the new owners about their plans

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble go behind the scenes at Longleat House and Safari Park once more. The keepers work hard to get the animals ready for winter, and also introduce new residents, including binturongs, dik-diks and ostriches

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: The Coastguard is called to rescue a paraglider who crashed into a cliff, while a special robot helps save a spaniel that had been stuck in a pipe for three days

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross trawls through the archives to pick the best festive buys from teams, and also learns all about the history of the Christmas dinner and pays a visit to a pantomime

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma's caught between helping an anxious bride-to-be or a mother in need of emergency housing

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who want to move from Surrey to Devon, hoping to find a sizeable home to fit three generations of their family and all for a budget of £800,000

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne rescues a collection of car exhaust parts, a workbench and a metal table frame, before he and two others restyle them in the hope of making a profit for the owners

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer takes on Catherine Southon as they search for items in the antiques shops along the south coast of England on their way to an auction in Leicestershire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to put the magic back into the festive season by practising a few conjuring tricks - but things do not always go according to plan and people keep disappearing. Eric and Cyril aim to persuade their wives to try Arkwrights' special French custard, while Mrs Rossi invites Mr Newbold round for Christmas lunch. What will the Black Widow have to say when she finds out? Comedy, starring David Jason, Johnny Vegas, Kulvinder Ghir, Geoffrey Whitehead, Sue Holderness and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush sees an opportunity and uses it to secure his future. Mick is pushed further away from Linda

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Local election fever hits the village and Geraldine finds she is a more popular candidate with the voters than over-confident parish council chairman David. Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: As lockdown drags on, Geraldine finds that getting a much-needed haircut from a friend may not always result in complete gorgeousness, especially if the scissors are held by someone rather more used to working with animals. Then, given an opportunity to share some time with some of the children from the local primary school, she finds that their knowledge of the miracles of Christ is somewhat muddled with that of Dynamo and Penn and Teller. Dawn French stars, with James Fleet

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past 11 months, the BBC's Medical Editor Fergus Walsh has followed the team at Oxford University and AstraZeneca as they designed, developed, manufactured and trialled their Covid-19 vaccine. In this programme he charts their progress, as well as scrutinisng the trial data and examining the vaccination's efficacy and safety

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Sammyjo hangs up a giant cuddle curtain despite the girls' scepticism, while Kendal has baby advice for Maddison, and there is an emergency vet visit for Boo the dog

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is an intriguing new arrival at the salon, a battered Mars bar gets a mixed reception and Sammyjo starts another creative project as the post-lockdown rush eases

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Sammyjo's back tattoo gets the laser treatment, the staff are hungover after a big night out, and there's talk of the 'L' word at the salon

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an extended edition of the quiz, with two guests joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Israeli actress Gal Gadot, who talks about new blockbuster Wonder Woman and catches up with 1984 Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman, whose debut book Quite is all about 'love, life and eyeliner'. Derry Girls star Nicola Coughlan chats about her involvement in American period drama Bridgerton and comedian Lee Mack discusses the return of his comedy series Not Going Out. With music from McFly, who perform their current single Tonight Is the Night

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit properties in Kent, Derby and Stoke-on-Trent, all of which have different renovation issues to resolve before they can be sold

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: New binturong Baloo is settling into its enclosure and awaiting a first meeting with a new mate, but the Christmas tryst does not go to plan as the boisterous animal escapes

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A man fights off masked house raiders to save his wife, a manta ray faces certain death if a fish hook is not removed, and in America, drivers stop to save a woman in a flash flood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presides at two antiques centres in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, as two teams of married couples go head to head. John Cameron leads the reds, while Caroline Hawley takes charge of the blues. The bargain hunters have to spend £300 on three items within an hour in the hope they'll make a profit at the auction in Suffolk. Meanwhile, Anita investigates the story behind an unusual toy

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie throws Lily a Bollywood-themed farewell party as Emma's plans for a romantic evening unravel and Rob and Karen try to convince Tom and Ella to accept help

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a Kent family find a new home in the East Riding of Yorkshire with room for a catering business, and takes a trip on a traditional fishing boat in Bridlington

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne use his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre in Monmouthshire, searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A collection of trampoline poles are worked on by carpenter Ollie Allen, while lighting specialist Guy Trench has to come up with a bright idea for a paraffin can and a pair of blow torches. For his own project, EJ has a large desk to keep him busy

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer finds some 18th-century porcelain, and Catherine Southon acquires some Scandinavian silver and samples an ancient pasty recipe before heading to an auction in Devon

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Revelations threaten to rock Stuart and Rainie. Tina unravels under the mounting pressure. Kim falls at the final hurdle while Denise insists on a traditional Christmas for Raymond

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian's commitment to going cold turkey is tested when Sarah Jane calls in a favour, and he finds himself tempted to crack under the pressure. Jodie feels pushed aside when Sacha's daughter starts asking questions about his surprise engagement, but refuses to give up without a fight. Nicky does not know who to believe when Chloe accuses Cameron of abusing his position

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Finals week gets underway with the last Invention Test of the year. The six remaining contestants are divided into two teams, one of which prepares rabbit in mustard sauce, while the other makes bouillabaisse with a rouille and a crouton. The chef who presents the finest dish in each of the groups will be rewarded with a pass straight through to the final four, while the others must make a classic dessert to avoid elimination

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: News of a terrible event reaches the US base in Chioggia, sending shock-waves of despair through the entire community and creating a faultline in Fraser and Caitlin's friendship

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, Caitlin prepares to leave the base and move back to Chicago with her family. But she and Fraser had made plans to sneak off to Blood Orange concert in Bologna together, and Fraser insists they should go ahead with the trip

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter W, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Sean Fletcher visit the Black Mountains, which straddle the border between Wales and England, to find out more about a drive to preserve ancient rural skills and traditions. Sean gets a bird's eye view of the spectacular landscape, while former thatcher Joe Sugg battles for supremacy in a hedge-laying competition. Joe Crowley investigates the challenges facing the Scottish salmon industry, and Adam Henson has high hopes for his new ram

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores Yorkshire by hot-air balloon. She meets two garden designers at Scampston Hall in Malton, and catches up with an old friend at York Gate Garden in Leeds. Plus, she learns about the Yorkshireman who became the father of aviation

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In July and August, the horticulturist deadheads the roses and stakes plants to help keep the garden looking its best during the summer months, and savours the full blooms of geraniums and lilies. She creates colourful borders using cannas, ginger and dahlias, before visiting her local beach, Braunton Burrows, to seek out native sea holly, which she hopes to grow herself

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits the ancestral home of his new gordon setter puppy - Gordon Castle in Moray - and Arlene Stuart joins the grooms training to care for race horses

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornish Christmas
programme.desc: Conclusion. The chef continues his celebrations in his adopted county of Cornwall, using local ingredients to prepare a Christmas banquet for his friends. These include comedian Jethro, an old acquaintance who used to be an adversary on the rugby pitch when they were younger

programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson conclude their special series for Advent exploring Britain's Christmas traditions, unwrapping how the season has been celebrated across the centuries. They discover why a ban on Christmas led to rioting in the streets, and how the festivities were revived with a vengeance in Victorian times. As they trace the stories behind now-common traditions - and take part in less familiar ones such as pub carolling - the Archbishop of Canterbury explains how he celebrates Christmas, and why its meaning remains so relevant today

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates food served to British monarchs from the time of George IV to the reign of the present Queen

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford joins Si and Dave as they demonstrate festive recipes using leftovers, including Christmas pudding trifle, bubble and squeak, tartiflette, gougere, and turkey pie

programme.title: Great Expectations (1946)
programme.desc: An orphan falls in love with the cold-hearted adopted daughter of an eccentric old woman. A mysterious benefactor gives him the chance to rise through London's high society, but in the process he forgets his humble roots and is not prepared for the truth about the girl he loves and the stranger funding his new life. Drama, based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring John Mills, Jean Simmons, Finlay Currie and Alec Guinness

programme.title: The Young Victoria (2009)
programme.desc: The turbulent early years of the monarch who, shortly after being crowned, became caught in a power struggle between her mother and scheming politicians Lord Melbourne and Sir Robert Peel. Meanwhile, a romance develops with her German cousin and eventual husband, Prince Albert. Historical drama, with Emily Blunt, Rupert Friend, Paul Bettany and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Katherine Higgins are at the Royal Marines Museum in Hampshire, where a 19th-century Japanese woodblock and a pristine pocket watch stimulate interest. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin learns how Portsmouth influenced the Victorian author Charles Dickens

programme.title: Golf: World Tour Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the season-ending European Tour event, held at Jemeirah Golf Estates in Dubai

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon discovers what happened to some of the most memorable businesses and entrepreneurs who have exited the famous Den doors over the past 17 series. Angela takes Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and new Dragon Sara Davies on a trip down memory lane when she shows them footage of themselves from former series, and she meets the latest, and youngest Dragon, Sara Davies, who has been a die-hard fan of the show since her university days

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the River Esk and Tees with his friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse. As well as trying to catch the glorious grayling, Bob is also going to try to ignite the joy of Christmas in Paul, who thinks the magic of the season has been long lost to commercialism

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Everyone Romesh has ever met has told him how amazing the Scottish islands are - but nobody he knows has actually ever been there. Now, in a year when travel abroad has been all but forbidden, he is heading to the Hebrides to find out if you can really have a proper adventure so close to home, spending a week visiting the isles of Skye, Harris and Lewis. Guiding him through the journey will be Donald Macsween, or Sweeney to his friends, a local crofter from the far north of Lewis - a place that lies as far north as Greenland. During their trip, Sweeney will show Romesh the spectacular rugged beauty of the islands and what at first glance look like Caribbean sandy beaches (but turn out to be utterly freezing). Rom will try his hand at the Highlands Games and try to avoid feeling up a sheep's testicles during a day spent on Sweeney's farm. Along the way, there will be a trip to the remote island of St Kilda - four hours out across the open Atlantic - and a chaotic afternoon spent with lo

programme.title: The Godfather (1972)
programme.desc: The patriarch of the Corleone crime family is hospitalised after an attempt on his life and entrusts control of his business to his three sons - the violent and unpredictable Sonny, the weak-willed Fredo, and Michael, the youngest brother, who reluctantly takes up the reins but proves to be the most ruthless of them all. Francis Ford Coppola's gangster drama, starring Marlon Brando, Al Pacino, James Caan, John Cazale, Talia Shire, Robert Duvall and Diane Keaton

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: When four villains lay siege to a bank, Angela sends Noah and Sylar after them. As Peter tries to avert a cataclysmic future, Hiro and Ando trace Daphne to Germany - but fall foul of the Haitian. Tracy Strauss leaves Washington in search of answers about Niki, Claire asks Meredith to teach her how to fight, and the results of Suresh's experiment take him by surprise

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter must embrace a dangerous ability to prevent a cataclysmic ending to a future where almost everyone has powers. After allowing Angela's half of the formula to be stolen, Hiro and Ando set out to unmask the villain who hired Daphne, seeking help from an unlikely source. As Noah gets rid of his new partner, Claire hits the road in pursuit of an enemy from her dad's old files. Meanwhile, a romance develops between two heroes, and Linderman and Molly return

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe and Cameron are driven further apart when they end up competing for the same job - and tension rises even further when Cameron deliberately sabotages his rival's chances. Skyler, Jeong and Josh work together as a team to save a gravely ill patient, under Henrik's watchful eye. Josh begins to see there is more to Skyler than he gave her credit for. Sacha and Jodie band together after their explosive domestic outburst

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at the best entertainment items bought by past teams, and watches some very famous theatre props fly at auction

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet head to south Wales. There's a mad dash around the shops as Serhat tries to eke out his tiny budget, while Christina struggles to blow all of her money. They look at baths, buckets and big sticks to try to turn a profit at the auction. Serhat follows in the footsteps of two brothers who discovered a whole underground world beneath their farmland, while a trip to a spa doesn't turn out quite as expected for Christina as she takes the plunge into a rather weird and wacky sport

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie heads to a friend's farm where a young calf has developed a serious limp. The calf's protective mother is making it clear she wants her baby back, and Jennie does not want to distress her, so has to work fast. Vet Darren treats a two-day-old lamb, whose owner thinks has a urinary infection. However, when she happily relieves herself all over Darren's examination table, he has to use all his investigative skills to diagnose the real problem. Maurice King, the founder of the Downe Veterinary Clinic, encounters a loggerhead turtle has been rescued by the local sea life sanctuary

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary heads to the Yorkshire coast to celebrate a unique bonfire party, explore the ancient seaport of Whitby and sample some classic, coastal comfort food - fish and chips and ice-cream. Mary visits a small smokehouse that has been run by the same family for five generations, where she helps smoke herring to make their world-renowned kippers. To end her time in Yorkshire, Mary helps a local community with their unique bonfire for Guy Fawkes Night. Inspired by all she sees, Mary makes a rustic smoked trout pate, followed by a one-pot haddock and shrimp feast

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Astrologer Russell Grant joins the duo as they reveal glamorous dishes able to impress any Christmas guests, including a recipe for turkey saltimbocca with game chips

programme.title: Scott of the Antarctic (1948)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following explorer Robert Falcon Scott's failed expedition to be the first to reach the South Pole. The extreme conditions, poor decision-making and the pressure of the race to make it to the Pole before their Norwegian rivals proved too much for the British team, resulting in tragedy in 1912. Starring John Mills, Derek Bond, James Robertson Justice, Kenneth More and John Gregson

programme.title: The Snow Wolf Family and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan hopes to get closer to wild wolves than anyone before to discover the true nature of this feared predator and heads to the north of Canada in search of a pack that has never seen people and so has no fear of humans. He begins to gain the trust of an Arctic wolf family and as time passes becomes accepted by the pack, including a female and its three newborn pups, and has some close encounters that reveal a softer side to the animals

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Paul Laidlaw hunt for items of interest in the picture galleries of the Bowes Museum at Barnard Castle in Co Durham, with finds including an illustrated book by Arthur Rackham and Edmund Dulac, and a carved dog's head

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the couple who missed out on the final after losing the dance-off. Plus, analysis of the weekend's dances and a look ahead to the contest's climax

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of John Singer Sargent, The Sopranos, Jane Austen's novels, and Nick Drake, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the opening second-round match as two teams compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Horseradish, Grape-nuts, Chinese chequers, and guinea pigs

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the quiz for students continues, with two teams representing their universities aiming to reach the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As Covid-19 infection rates rise again, the Royal Free London prepares to roll out a mass vaccination programme. However, the trusts faces a staffing crisis due to an outbreak of coronavirus on three of the Barnet wards, which in turn threatens vital cancer operations

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by singer Alexandra Burke and comedian Chris Ramsey to take a look at the year's highlights, including Barack Obama becoming America's first black president and Gwyneth Paltrow's launching of lifestyle brand Goop. Alexandra also reflects on winning The X Factor that year and how it felt to share a stage with Beyoncé

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: The second documentary looks at how Britain coped with the loss of empire, the dominance of America and the rise of a consolidated European superstate in the decades after the Second World War. Andrew Marr profiles some of the key figures who sought to come to terms with Britain's decline, including Earl Mountbatten of Burma, cookery writer Elizabeth David and singer and activist Bob Geldof, as well as less familiar figures like Colonel Colin 'Mad Mitch' Mitchell, and pioneering peace campaigner Helen John

programme.title: Viceroy's House (2017)
programme.desc: The final Viceroy of India, Lord Mountbatten, oversees the transition of British India to independence, but meets with conflict as opposing sides clash in the face of huge change. Drama, starring Hugh Bonneville, Gillian Anderson and Manish Dayal

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Sean Fletcher visit the Black Mountains, which straddle the border between Wales and England, to find out more about a drive to preserve ancient rural skills and traditions. Sean gets a bird's eye view of the spectacular landscape, while former thatcher Joe Sugg battles for supremacy in a hedge-laying competition. Joe Crowley investigates the challenges facing the Scottish salmon industry, and Adam Henson has high hopes for his new ram

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The barrister helps Noemie Lopian find out how her French mother survived the war after she was arrested as a child by the Nazis, meeting the grandson of the man who hid her mother and siblings in a chicken shed in his garden during round-ups of Jews in the town of Saint-Junien. Noemie also learns of the incredible sacrifice and courage of a young female French resistance fighter who saved her mother's life

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four chefs competing in this round are challenged to prepare either potato rosti served with egg, bacon and tomatoes or tempura mussels with a smoked garlic aioli for their skills test. For the second round, they have one hour and 25 minutes to cook their own two-course signature dishes and secure themselves a place in the quarter finals

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble go behind the scenes at Longleat House and Safari Park once more. The keepers work hard to get the animals ready for winter, and also introduce new residents, including binturongs, dik-diks and ostriches

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer takes on Catherine Southon as they search for items in the antiques shops along the south coast of England on their way to an auction in Leicestershire

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a physics student, a facilities manager and a customer advisor must choose between working together to pool their knowledge or going it alone

programme.title: King of Kings (1961)
programme.desc: Biblical epic chronicling the life of Christ, from the rumours of a coming Messiah before his birth, through his rise as a spiritual leader to his eventual betrayal, crucifixion and resurrection. Narrated by Orson Welles and starring Jeffrey Hunter, Siobhan McKenna, Robert Ryan, Rip Torn, Hurd Hatfield and Harry Guardino

programme.title: The Snow Wolf Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to Canada in the autumn to reunite with the Arctic wolves he has been following as they try to raise their pups and prepare for winter. The cameraman also walks with members of the pack as they hunt lemmings, musk oxen and hares and encounter a rival pack

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Memorable moments and previously unseen finds, including a handkerchief commemorating cricket legend Dr WG Grace and three postcard albums found on a council tip

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball talks to the couple who managed to avoid elimination at the weekend and took the last spot in the final. Plus, a closer look at the weekend's performances in Choreography Corner, and a few previous winners join Zoe to review the series

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace, Cherry Healey and Ruth Goodman visit places where traditional Christmas items roll off the production line. Gregg goes to Britain's biggest cake factory, where 180 million mince pies are produced each year, and pitches in on the yule log conveyor belt. Cherry finds out where tinsel and wrapping paper are made and Ruth learns the history of Christmas crackers

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Simon Rogan and Tommy Banks join the desserts and petit fours round, in which the eight contestants prepare dishes including edible snow globes, mince pies, baked Alaska, and a new taker on the classic Christmas selection box. They must also judge each other's efforts in a blind taste test. Former judge Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: Highlights from past Christmas episodes of the panel show, with host Sandi Toksvig and regular panellist Alan Davies

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Harper reveals details of Eric's indiscretions to Daria, setting in motion a chain of events with serious ramifications. Yasmin tries to get Maxim to come to the Christmas party, but he struggles to see past what Kenny did at the strip club. Greg is left in an unstable state after being publicly humiliated, and his state of mind soon escalates when Usman shows up with some grade A narcotics

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A selection of hit performances from the 1960s to the present day

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: During late summer and autumn, the Royal Free London has so far managed to remain open to all its patients and continue with planned operations, treatment and care. However, the second wave of the pandemic has now reached London, and confirmed cases of the virus are prompting a fresh influx of patients with Covid-19 into the hospitals. As a second England-wide lockdown is in force, the Royal Free Hospital must continue to try to get through the biggest operating waiting list in its history. As a specialist centre, it sees some of the UK's most complicated cancer patients. But with many patients in need of intensive care beds, the pressure placed on the Trust's intensive care staff is immense. Meanwhile, in Barnet, the trust's second largest hospital, the number of Covid-19 patients has more than doubled in the last week. As the hospital deals with the new influx of patients, Barnet's senior team have a major staffing problem. Because of an outbreak of Covid-19 on three of the wards, st

programme.title: Their Finest (2016)
programme.desc: Former secretary Catrin Cole, newly appointed by the British Ministry of Information as a scriptwriter for propaganda films, joins the cast and crew of a major production while the Blitz rages around them. Comedy drama, starring Gemma Arterton, Sam Claflin, Richard E Grant and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In October 1917, the Bolsheviks rose up, swept the Russian tsar from power and communism was born. However, the exact facts of the Russian Revolution are disputed. Here, the historian finds it was really a group of women workers who kick-started the event nine months earlier. At the time, the Bolsheviks tried to stop it, and Lenin - radical leader of the Bolsheviks - wasn't even in the country. Lucy also discovers that the tsar was forced to abdicate long before the Bolsheviks took control and that King George V betrayed his cousin by opposing the British government's offer of asylum to the tsar

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. First, Monica asks two chefs to fillet and cook john dory and serve with a vibrant green parsley sauce and a garlic cream, before the second pair of competitors make poached figs with honeycomb and a flavoured cream for Marcus. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham finds out about the much-loved dolphin that has started breaking people's bones and reveals how a tiny crustacean has mastered the holy grail of science - the art of invisibility

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary footage of the volcanic fires of Iceland and the creatures that make their home amid this seemingly inhospitable terrain. The film was shot during the country's most dramatic eruption in years, in which a huge volcano exploded under the icecap, melting billions of tonnes of ice

programme.title: Billy Connolly: A Scot in the Arctic
programme.desc: The comic heads for the North Pole, braving sub-zero temperatures and dealing with a persistent film crew to experience life in Canada's far North West. His only other company are the inquisitive polar bears who occasionally pay him a visit. First shown in 1995

programme.title: The Last Igloo
programme.desc: Documentary following a day in the life of an Inuit hunter in Greenland, who builds one of the traditional ice dwellings while fishing through the sea ice. While igloos are no longer used as permanent homes, the traditional skills behind their construction are still used in building temporary shelters in the wilderness. The film captures this ingenious craft, which may be lost to the world as the ice sheet disappears

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott and Maggie Aderin-Pocock consider the state of astronomy in 2020 and wonder what new, exciting discoveries await across the rest of the decade

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Thanks to NASA's Juno mission, scientists are starting to peel back the layers of Jupiter to reveal the wonders within. Professor Kaitlin Kratter reveals how extreme the planet is, with the aid of a quarry and a few rocks, giving some idea of the size of the second largest body in the solar system. And at the National Ignition facility in Northern California, Dr Marius Millot uses powerful lasers normally used for nuclear fusion for an experiment giving an idea of the pressures inside Jupiter. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Holst: The Planets with Professor Brian Cox
programme.desc: A chance to see the BBC Symphony Orchestra perform British composer Gustav Holst's The Planets at the London Barbican, in a concert from last September timed to celebrate the centenary of the premiere. But there's a special scientific twist to this concert, as before each movement, Professor Brian Cox discusses what modern science reveals about each of the planets

programme.title: My Week with Marilyn (2011)
programme.desc: A film student gets a job as a production assistant on the set of movie The Prince and the Showgirl. He bears witness to the clash of conflicting personalities between leads Marilyn Monroe and Laurence Olivier, and becomes a confidant of the troubled, lonely Hollywood actress. Fact-based drama, starring Michelle Williams, Eddie Redmayne, Kenneth Branagh, Judi Dench and Emma Watson

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Clovelly in Devon, looking at its transformation from fishing harbour to romantic Victorian seaside resort. Thanks to a powerful and pioneering woman, Clovelly found itself at the forefront of the holiday revolution, and even today cars are banned and the village is privately owned, helping to preserve it as a reminder of a bygone age

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The first of a selection of programmes in which American artist Bob Ross creates winter scenes, beginning with one in which soft colours flow from his brushes

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams made up of distinguished graduates of Trinity Hall, Cambridge and St Peter's College, Oxford compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. Among those facing Jeremy Paxman's questions are actress Sophie Winkleman and journalist Lucy Mangan for Trinity, with BBC security correspondent Gordon Corera and broadcaster Afua Hirsch representing St Peter's

programme.title: The Art Mysteries with Waldemar Januszczak
programme.desc: The art critic explores the hidden meanings in Seurat's little-known painting Les Poseuses, a depiction of three nudes in an artist's studio which is currently hanging in the Barnes Foundation in Philadelphia. He tries to ascertain why the artist chose to put an image of one of his more famous works, A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte, in the background

programme.title: The Lady in the Van (2015)
programme.desc: The true story of writer Alan Bennett's friendship with an eccentric homeless woman, whom he befriended in the 1970s before allowing her to park her dilapidated Bedford van outside his Camden home, where she ended up staying for 15 years. Comedy-drama, starring Maggie Smith, Alex Jennings, Roger Allam and Jim Broadbent

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Diaries
programme.desc: A candid profile of the writer, following him in his home village in Yorkshire, to his community library in Primrose Hill and during a trip to New York. In a series of intimate encounters filmed over the period of a year, he reflects on his modest beginnings and his enduring gratitude to a Welfare State that paid for his education and looked after his parents in their old age

programme.title: Civilisations
programme.desc: In a companion programme to Civilisations, classicist Mary Beard goes in search of extraordinary works of art from all over the world that can be seen in Britain, from Roman marbles and Egyptian mummies to Renaissance masterpieces and African sculptures

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A profile of the groundbreaking figure skater, who won Olympic gold in 1976 and whose introduction of balletic moves and focus on presentation transformed the sport. This documentary features previously unseen footage of some of Curry's remarkable performances, along with archive interviews with the man himself and of those closest to him

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The first of a selection of programmes in which American artist Bob Ross creates winter scenes, beginning with one in which soft colours flow from his brushes

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian concludes his review by looking at the last few years of the decade and a society transformed by accelerated change. Not only did the late 1980s see the rise of the yuppie and the economic big bang in the City of London, but shopping habits changed with the growing popularity of German cars, French wine, Italian fashion and Scandinavian interior design. Alongside this transformation was a growing disconnection from the political elite, signified by the growing rave culture and the community charge riots. For Margaret Thatcher, widely seen as the architect of much of this change, things would not end well

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman chairs the latest round of the celebrity quiz, which pits famous alumni of Warwick University and Imperial College London against each other. Representing Warwick are actor Tom Goodman-Hill and The World Tonight presenter Ritula Shah, while the Imperial team is engineer Brendan Walker and writer and broadcaster Kenan Malik

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a winter scene, using shades of indigo create the stark reality of the season

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Hacker attempts to gain support for his tax cuts - by threatening to raise the duty on tobacco. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: A territorial battle develops in the corridors of power. Sir Humphrey has skilfully moved the PM's political adviser Dorothy Wainwright out of her office - but she insists on moving back. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary looks at how Margaret Thatcher's leadership style and the issues of Europe and the poll tax split her cabinet and created public anger, leading to her downfall. Protests against the poll tax resulted in violence in central London, but more damagingly there was widespread disenchantment in Tory strongholds across the country. Fractures emerge with senior cabinet colleagues, worsened by her European policy and Geoffrey Howe's resignation leads to a leadership challenge

programme.title: Great Speeches
programme.desc: Conservative MP Geoffrey Howe's influential addressing of the House of Commons in 1990, 12 days after his resignation from the government, a speech many believe shook the nation and precipitated the downfall of Margaret Thatcher

programme.title: Diary Days
programme.desc: An insight into life on November 22, 1990, when Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher resigned

programme.title: Favourite Things: Margaret Thatcher
programme.desc: Russell Harty meets the former prime minister to discuss her life, interests and personal possessions

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: Mark Kermode celebrates one of the most perennial of all movie genres - the Christmas film. He unwraps a glittering selection of festive cinematic treats, from Hollywood blockbusters to family favourites, revealing the techniques and storytelling secrets that make them so successful. Touching on styles ranging from fairy-tale fantasy to high-octane action, family drama to horror, Mark considers how the best of these films are able to capture something magical - the Christmas spirit. But how do they do it?

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary asking why the festive meal remains the nation's last great traditional feast, and if it can continue to stand the test of time in an increasingly diverse society. With contributions by actor Simon Callow, food writer Prue Leith, cultural critic Jonathan Meades and historians Kate Colquhoun and Kathryn Hughes

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata and Hakon continue their investigation in Borgarnes, where the chief of police at the time of Tomas's disappearance admits mistakes were made. Back in Reykjavik, Arnar discovers he has missed two telephone calls from Fannar Thorsson. Crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Hakon calls from Borgarnes, furious that Kata did not tell him that she had escalated the referral about the Valhalla investigation. Unperturbed, Kata is determined to find justice for the victims. Crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: What is it like when your mum becomes your dad? Twelve-year-old Tilly’s mum came out as transgender when she was 7 years old. We follow Tilly as she shows us what her life is like now. Having just moved up to secondary school, Tilly hasn't told her new friends about her family as she is uncertain as to when will be the right time to tell them. When her birthday party comes up, Tilly has a decision to make - will she explain to her friends that Jack is trans or will she make up a story to buy herself more time? To help her decide, Tilly meets Nat and Faith, who both have trans dads and help her feel more confident about sharing her story. And when younger brother Mal experiences some mean comments at school, new friend Nat offers to give a talk to his class.

programme.desc: he Go Jetters are in snowy Norway to learn about the reindeer that are cared for by the local Sami people. In the spring the reindeer go on a very long journey called migration and as part of this journey they catch a ferry to take them to their warmer summer home. Grandmaster Glitch and the Grimbots are also in Norway, but their Grimbler scares the migrating reindeer and some of them run away from the herd. The Go Jetters need to keep them calm and get them back to the herd so they can finish their migration

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

Zainab and Kobi decide to make sure Alex is okay, and they discover that Alex is having to look after her mum who's just been released from hospital. programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: In this winter special, Ferne travels to Norway to find out about animals that live in very cold climates. She meets Joar and his herd of reindeer and together they visit a polar park. They find out how animals adapt to live in cold winter conditions.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: It's Christmas Eve and a mysterious box arrives at Josh, Zac and Nathan's house! The brothers open it to find some new Christmas pyjamas, and letters from Santa himself! Before heading to bed the brothers prepare some late night snacks for Santa and his reindeer. On Christmas Day the boys wake up early and can't wait to see if Santa has been. Later, the whole family has a Christmas sing-along with Mum at the piano.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie finds out how a snail moves and discovers how a tractor is made in a factory.

programme.title: Swashbuckle

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is having a bad hair day, and the Squirrels may not be able to help him

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter, Benjamin and Lily are tempted by a mysterious hoard of radishes, Lily finds herself caught in Mr Tod's trap. Can Peter find a way to rescue Lily before Tod reaches her?

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to Central America in order to learn the dance of a bird called manakin

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grim HQ's heating gets stuck on, Glitch looks for a cold place to land and cool down. Meanwhile, the Go Jetters enjoy the cool sights of Harbin Ice City in China. Every year sculptors at the festival create realistic models of world famous landmarks from blocks of ice and Lars is attempting to carve the Eiffel Tower

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: In this episode we follow Rose as she celebrates Christmas with her family. Rose and her family visit the garden centre, where they find lots of festive decorations which they purchase alongside a Christmas tree. Once they arrive back at home, they all work together to decorate the house. After putting up the tree and leaving a sign for Santa in the window, Rose's parents take her for a magical experience: visiting Santa Claus in his grotto. Here, Rose tells Santa all about how well behaved she has been this year. Before leaving, Rose says goodbye to Santa and his reindeers, which are all outside in the snow. When Christmas Eve finally arrives, Rose attends a Christmas carol service with her family. Here they learn about the story of Jesus and sing some carols together. They head home to put on their festive pyjamas before going to bed. The next morning it's Christmas Day, and the family wake up to their presents under the tree. After the family give each other their gifts, they sit do

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Jack tells the story of his quest to find three Christmas gifts, which turned out to have a huge significance on his life

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah wants some lights for her Christmas tree, and Bug knows where she might find a set

programme.title: Tee and Mo Song Time
programme.desc: Bag Full of Love is a heartfelt song about a mother's love for her child. Like all mothers, Mo takes care everyday to pack her bag full off all of the things her boy might need for the day. However, she also knows that sometimes the thing Tee needs most is a hug from his mum.

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Lambkin must catch the tub wen it rolls away with Nana inside it

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Cloud has been on holiday in space, but when it returns the Clangers notice it is very cold. Mother Clanger is worried when she sees it snowing rather than raining, and she calls for Major Clanger to come and have a look. The Clangers have never seen snow before. Major Clanger finds it very interesting and decides to take a sample to his workshop to analyse, but he's amazed to see that it has disappeared and has been replaced by water. While Small Clanger and Baby Soup Dragon enjoy a snowball fight, Tiny Clanger spots The Cloud shivering and comes up with a clever plan. Will Tiny's plan be enough to stop The Cloud shivering?

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos ride on the Pinky Ponk, but it bumps into a tree and the gazebo. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple in their search for their perfect retirement retreat in the Cornish countryside on a budget of £400,000. Jules also visits the National Lobster Hatchery to lend a hand in their conservation work

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Ellesmere Port, where Rachael, Lyndsey and daughter Missy-Mae want a garden to match their fun-loving personalities

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads off on two contrasting walks, one down by the sea and the other up in the hills. He begins by taking a hop across the water from Tenby to Caldey Island. Well-known for its monastery, monks and lavender perfume, it's also ideal for a sunny stroll. He then takes a circular walk around Carreg Cennen, one of the most dramatic, photogenic and romantic castles in Wales

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen looks at the natural world in Wales, featuring archive clips of horse whisperers, pet crocodiles and Welsh moonshine

programme.title: Wales and the History of the World
programme.desc: Eddie Butler examines the symbols and ideas that represent Wales, from the red dragon and the red flag to ancient burial mounds and the iPod. He also learns about the Welsh influence in an Indian community, and meets the man who helped enlighten 'dark satanic mills' around the world

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past 11 months, the BBC's Medical Editor Fergus Walsh has followed the team at Oxford University and AstraZeneca as they designed, developed, manufactured and trialled their Covid-19 vaccine. programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Sammyjo hangs up a giant cuddle curtain despite the girls' scepticism, while Kendal has baby advice for Maddison, and there is an emergency vet visit for Boo the dog

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is an intriguing new arrival at the salon, a battered Mars bar gets a mixed reception and Sammyjo starts another creative project as the post-lockdown rush eases

programme.title: Children's Ward
programme.desc: A boy is rushed into Children's A&E covered in a rash. Meningitis is ruled out and the medical team suspects disease so rare that most of the doctors have never seen it before. A girl is brought in after swallowing a split pin, leaving doctors with the choice of intervening or allowing it to pass through her naturally. It is an important day for a girl who previously suffered an allergic reaction to peanuts

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells

programme.desc: The animals in countries on the northern edges of the Atlantic Ocean face worsening weather in autumn and the first stirrings of winter. programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford, Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa at Molineux and Newcastle United v West Bromwich Albion at St James' Park

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the third Sunday of Advent, Father John Dickson, Catholic Chaplain, leads the ecumenical service from the Chapel of Royal Holloway, University of London. With hymns and carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a new recording from the University's Chapel Choir

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm as the hunt heads to Lincoln, with Charlie Ross and Richard Madley helping the teams, as Eric learns about one of Lincoln's most famous sons

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Thursford in Norfolk to experience a Christmas light show and walks through the four-acre lantern extravaganza. Katie Piper visits a church in North London where their Christmas Angel hampers bring some festive cheer to families in need. Featuring favourite Christmas carols with performances from Collabro, Marisha Wallace, and opera star Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple in their search for their perfect retirement retreat in the Cornish countryside on a budget of £400,000. Jules also visits the National Lobster Hatchery to lend a hand in their conservation work

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Ellesmere Port, where Rachael, Lyndsey and daughter Missy-Mae want a garden to match their fun-loving personalities

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore puts in some hard graft at the nation's tips to uncover four items that can be transformed. Master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, upholsterer Anthony Devine and design guru Jay Blades are on hand to help reinvent the pieces of furniture into desirable pieces. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The semi-final results show opens with a routine from the Strictly professionals, and there's also a musical performance from pop superstars Little Mix. More importantly, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman also reveal which two couples will compete in the dance off, leaving the judges to decide who will not be making it through to the final

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra and Will find allies who can help them in their search for Will's father, the Magisterium learn something shocking, and Mrs Coulter meets a formidable foe. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: When 12-year-old Kingsley is transferred to a special-needs school, a group of West Indian women uncover an unofficial segregation policy preventing many black children from receiving the education they deserve. Co-writer/director Steve McQueen's drama, starring Kenyah Sandy and Sharlene Whyte

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman and guests review the day's Premier League action, featuring Fulham v Liverpool at Craven Cottage, Crystal Palace v Tottenham Hotspur at Selhurst Park, Arsenal v Burnley at Emirates Stadium, Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at King Power Stadium and Southampton v Sheffield United at St Mary's Stadium

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to put the magic back into the festive season by practising a few conjuring tricks - but things do not always go according to plan and people keep disappearing. Eric and Cyril aim to persuade their wives to try Arkwrights' special French custard, while Mrs Rossi invites Mr Newbold round for Christmas lunch. What will the Black Widow have to say when she finds out? Comedy, starring David Jason, Johnny Vegas, Kulvinder Ghir, Geoffrey Whitehead, Sue Holderness and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush sees an opportunity and uses it to secure his future. Mick is pushed further away from Linda

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: As lockdown drags on, Geraldine finds that getting a much-needed haircut from a friend may not always result in complete gorgeousness, especially if the scissors are held by someone rather more used to working with animals. Then, given an opportunity to share some time with some of the children from the local primary school, she finds that their knowledge of the miracles of Christ is somewhat muddled with that of Dynamo and Penn and Teller. programme.title: Dana - The Original Derry Girl
programme.desc: The story of the singer, who won the 1970 Eurovision Song Contest for Ireland with All Kinds of Everything. At a time when the violent conflict of the Troubles was dominating the news, Rosemary Scallon - better known as Dana - became a national treasure overnight while still a teenager. The programme looks at what happened after she won the competition, including her successful pop and TV career in the 70s, her marriage to Newry hotelier Damien Scallon, her move to Alabama, and her stint in Irish politics

programme.desc: This year's Nobel Peace Prize has been awarded to the UN agency dedicated to feeding the hungry and fending off mass starvation, the World Food Programme. programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: On the 70th anniversary of one of her career-defining films All about Eve, Talking Movies looks back at the life and times of Bette Davis, regarded as one of the greatest actresses in Hollywood history. Davis worked for more than 60 years and made over 100 films, including classics such as Whatever Happened to Baby Jane? and Dark Victory. She was a trailblazer for the independent actress who feuded with studio bosses at a time when women in Hollywood were not expected to be assertive. The programme includes of one of the last major interviews Davis gave on a film set to Talking Movies host Tom Brook, filmed on location in Maine where she was shooting her penultimate film, The Whales of August, which co-starred silent screen icon Lillian Gish. This tribute to Davis also includes interviews with younger actresses who still regard Davis as an inspirational force and an assessment of her body of work from a range of critics, film-makers, producers and her many fans. programme.title: Bill W
programme.desc: Time Magazine named Bill Wilson, the founder of Alcoholics Anonymous, one of its 100 Heroes and Icons of the 20th century for the legacy he left behind in helping millions find an answer not only to alcoholism, but to many other forms of addiction that destroy lives all around the world. The film follows Wilson through his 17 years of alcoholism, the events that led to his own sobriety, the crucial years of AA's founding and growth, his battle with depression, his experimentation with LSD, and his struggle with celebrity status in an anonymous society
