programme.desc: Weasel tries for his AeroFizz pilot's license. Magoo ruins everything when, convinced he's fixing his roof, he pierces a hole in the airship.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel shoot people with toy arrows to make them fall in love with Fizz. But Magoo mistakes them for a giant hornet.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The heroes of International Rescue must deal with emergencies on two fronts when an orbiting solar energy station malfunctions, placing a passing airliner - and its precious cargo - in peril

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Can Danny handle the pressure of performing basketball tricks in front of hundreds of fans and avoid the messy forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs. Wicket demands that Bean stay in to take delivery of a new lounge chair. He is forced to dress up as Mrs. Wicket to make sure it is delivered!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean is charged with keeping an eye on Irma's dog Mopsy, but Mopsy is a very wilful pooch! Who is really in charge of who?

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series serving as a prequel to the popular film series. Dr Gillman is discovered to be a fraud.

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Having just moved to the boring Metropolis suburbs, Batgirl is thrilled when she intercepts a call from Batman asking for help.

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Join Vick Hope, Sam Homewood, Frankie Vu and Fred Dinenage as they ask more questions beginning with How. Things get gross as they discover how the colour blue came from the loo, Frankie's in for a surprise when Vick demonstrates just how strong hair is, and Fred's feeling arty as he challenges the team to paint a quick pic.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans have to stop Darkseid's latest scheme

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: From the Domesday village of Symondsbury, the expert walker follows the ancient in-land drovers route, including a climb up the iconic Colmer's Hill. Her journey takes her through the Dorset Holloways, and culminates with a climb to Golden Cap - the highest point on the south coast. Her trek ends with a meal of fish and chips in a pub with sea views across Lyme Bay

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh this week are Beverley Knight and Cliff Richard, who is toasting eight decades at the top with festive liqueurs

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Alex Beresford and Ria Hebden visit the country's most fascinating attractions, sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Hull to transform the garden of a 90-year-old land girl and ballroom dancing devotee. Frailty has seen the twice-widowed great-grandmother lose the ability to tend her once beautiful gardens, so they have become neglected no-go areas. After Alan surprises her at a tea dance, the team transforms her gardens into spaces she can safely tend to again

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart

programme.title: Shrek (2001)
programme.desc: Anti-social ogre Shrek resolves to stop an evil ruler from banishing fairy-tale characters to his swampland home. However, the reluctant hero ends up forced to rescue the beautiful Princess Fiona from a castle guarded by a dragon, aided only by a wisecracking donkey. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel

programme.title: The Grinch (2018)
programme.desc: In the magical town of Whoville, a race of human-like creatures called Whos are thrilled about celebrating Christmas. However, a cantankerous, furry green loner named the Grinch, who lives in a cave north of Whoville, hatches a dastardly scheme to ruin the festive season. Animated Dr Seuss adaptation, with the voices of Benedict Cumberbatch, Pharrell Williams and Rashida Jones

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: Pat Gibson, current number one in the world quiz rankings, recalls the highs and lows of the game that made him a millionaire in 2004. Plus, a look at memorable moments from the games of celebrities Al Murray, Harry Hill, Paul O'Grady, Jo Brand and Nick Hancock

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... A Castle Story
programme.desc: Ant and Dec and the cast of 2020 take a look back at the highs and lows of their experience. Cameras focus on some of those tasks which pushed the likes of Jordan North, Shane Richie and Vernon Kay to their limits. Plus, a look at Giovanna Fletcher's success as Queen of the Castle

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des O'Connor went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The former EastEnders actress tells Piers why she decided to stop playing Peggy Mitchell, landlady of the Queen Vic - a role she was associated with for 14 years. The London-born star also discusses her rise to fame in the Carry On film series, beginning with Carry On Spying, and talks about her private life, which has seen her marry three times

programme.title: Coronation Street: 60 Unforgettable Years
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley narrates a celebration of the diamond anniversary of the programme, exploring the legacy of the world's longest-running TV soap opera. There are revelations from the show's stars past and present, including audition nerves, fallings out, memorable location shoots, as well as their memories of the storylines that gripped the nation

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... A Castle Story
programme.desc: Ant and Dec and the cast of 2020 take a look back at the highs and lows of their experience. Cameras focus on some of those tasks which pushed the likes of Jordan North, Shane Richie and Vernon Kay to their limits. Plus, a look at Giovanna Fletcher's success as Queen of the Castle

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Thruxton, with racing from the Mini Challenge Trophy and Round 11 of the Ginetta Juniors

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Stoke-on-Trent, where Debbie Serpell works hard to get hold of an elephant foot stool and Fay Rutter's bids for a diamond and ruby ring do not go as planned

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five family members answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. programme.title: ITV News at Ten

programme.title: Death Row's Women with Susanna Reid
programme.desc: The Good Morning Britain presenter travels to Texas, home of the most active death chamber in the United States, to meet death row inmate Darlie Routier. Twenty three years ago, the Dallas housewife was convicted and sentenced to death by legal injection for killing one of her sons, and remains accused of murdering the other - despite there being no eyewitnesses, no confession and no motive. Darlie denies the murders and decades on has refused a route off death row in exchange for accepting her guilt. Susanna delves into the history of the case, meeting key witnesses and family members, to find out if the convict is as innocent as she claims

programme.desc: The series concludes with tips on buying food and drink, starting with alternatives to turkey for Christmas dinner - including meat, fish and vegetarian options. programme.title: Queens of the Street

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz is at the beach building a sandcastle. Magoo thinks they are preparing to thwart an attack by an enemy riding a dragon.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tries for his AeroFizz pilot's license. Magoo ruins everything when, convinced he's fixing his roof, he pierces a hole in the airship.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel shoot people with toy arrows to make them fall in love with Fizz. But Magoo mistakes them for a giant hornet.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The heroes of International Rescue must deal with emergencies on two fronts when an orbiting solar energy station malfunctions, placing a passing airliner - and its precious cargo - in peril

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Can Danny handle the pressure of performing basketball tricks in front of hundreds of fans and avoid the messy forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs. Wicket demands that Bean stay in to take delivery of a new lounge chair. He is forced to dress up as Mrs. Wicket to make sure it is delivered!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean is charged with keeping an eye on Irma's dog Mopsy, but Mopsy is a very wilful pooch! Who is really in charge of who?

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series serving as a prequel to the popular film series. Dr Gillman is discovered to be a fraud.

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Having just moved to the boring Metropolis suburbs, Batgirl is thrilled when she intercepts a call from Batman asking for help.

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Join Vick Hope, Sam Homewood, Frankie Vu and Fred Dinenage as they ask more questions beginning with How. Things get gross as they discover how the colour blue came from the loo, Frankie's in for a surprise when Vick demonstrates just how strong hair is, and Fred's feeling arty as he challenges the team to paint a quick pic.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans have to stop Darkseid's latest scheme

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: From the Domesday village of Symondsbury, the expert walker follows the ancient in-land drovers route, including a climb up the iconic Colmer's Hill. Her journey takes her through the Dorset Holloways, and culminates with a climb to Golden Cap - the highest point on the south coast. Her trek ends with a meal of fish and chips in a pub with sea views across Lyme Bay

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh this week are Beverley Knight and Cliff Richard, who is toasting eight decades at the top with festive liqueurs

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Alex Beresford and Ria Hebden visit the country's most fascinating attractions, sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Hull to transform the garden of a 90-year-old land girl and ballroom dancing devotee. Frailty has seen the twice-widowed great-grandmother lose the ability to tend her once beautiful gardens, so they have become neglected no-go areas. After Alan surprises her at a tea dance, the team transforms her gardens into spaces she can safely tend to again

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart

programme.title: Shrek (2001)
programme.desc: Anti-social ogre Shrek resolves to stop an evil ruler from banishing fairy-tale characters to his swampland home. However, the reluctant hero ends up forced to rescue the beautiful Princess Fiona from a castle guarded by a dragon, aided only by a wisecracking donkey. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel

programme.title: The Grinch (2018)
programme.desc: In the magical town of Whoville, a race of human-like creatures called Whos are thrilled about celebrating Christmas. However, a cantankerous, furry green loner named the Grinch, who lives in a cave north of Whoville, hatches a dastardly scheme to ruin the festive season. Animated Dr Seuss adaptation, with the voices of Benedict Cumberbatch, Pharrell Williams and Rashida Jones

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: Pat Gibson, current number one in the world quiz rankings, recalls the highs and lows of the game that made him a millionaire in 2004. Plus, a look at memorable moments from the games of celebrities Al Murray, Harry Hill, Paul O'Grady, Jo Brand and Nick Hancock

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... A Castle Story
programme.desc: Ant and Dec and the cast of 2020 take a look back at the highs and lows of their experience. Cameras focus on some of those tasks which pushed the likes of Jordan North, Shane Richie and Vernon Kay to their limits. Plus, a look at Giovanna Fletcher's success as Queen of the Castle

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des O'Connor went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The former EastEnders actress tells Piers why she decided to stop playing Peggy Mitchell, landlady of the Queen Vic - a role she was associated with for 14 years. The London-born star also discusses her rise to fame in the Carry On film series, beginning with Carry On Spying, and talks about her private life, which has seen her marry three times

programme.title: Coronation Street: 60 Unforgettable Years
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley narrates a celebration of the diamond anniversary of the programme, exploring the legacy of the world's longest-running TV soap opera. There are revelations from the show's stars past and present, including audition nerves, fallings out, memorable location shoots, as well as their memories of the storylines that gripped the nation

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... A Castle Story

programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Thruxton, with racing from the Mini Challenge Trophy and Round 11 of the Ginetta Juniors

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tim intends to make up for lost time with Elaine, Maria begs Faye to report Ray to the police, and Todd manages to win Billy's trust

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy struggles to contact the store's new parent company, Sandra needs help to stand up to Carol, Glenn wrestles with a big decision, and Jonah takes up the cause of gender equality

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: The employees wrestle with the loss of beloved co-worker Myrtle - and the fact that she left a large sum of money to Jonah. Plus, Dina reconnects with a surprise visitor

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress rugby player Jakob in Cardiff, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes he liked best

programme.title: Love Bites
programme.desc: Love Bites will see three suitors battle it out to win a date by each cooking a three-course meal, using the same box of ingredients.

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel and Jai speak to the kids. Tracy has a plan to reunite Leyla and Liam. Wendy is delighted by Victoria's offer, but Bob is disappointed

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Faye announces that she has quit her job, Debbie asks Gary to reconsider his position, and Todd goes snooping in Paul and Billy's flat

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tim intends to make up for lost time with Elaine, Maria begs Faye to report Ray to the police, and Todd manages to win Billy's trust

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Ellen is joined by actor and singer Leslie Odom Jr, who performs Last Christmas from his album The Christmas Album. Plus, Ellen has more to give to lucky winners in Ellen's 12 Days of Giveaways.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons attempt to impress cheerleader Lily Amelia with their choice of outfit from Leeds city centre - which of them will be successful and be chosen for a prized date?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in the ultimate shopping challenge, as they try to win the affections of picky photographer Danielle by each buying her an outfit

programme.title: Love Bites
programme.desc: Love Bites will see three suitors battle it out to win a date by each cooking a three-course meal, using the same box of ingredients.

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates a selection of festive camcorder calamities and mobile phone footage, featuring rocking around the Christmas tree and five ways to kill a snowman

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a festive-themed selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring clips of a photo-bombing reindeer, an explosive chimney and a squirrel wrecking his neighbour's Christmas lights. Plus, there's an embarrassing moment when a robotic Santa exposes himself

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden hopes to create the perfect Christmas experience for Louis, who has lost his faith in the festivities, but Alan's cheapness - as well as his mother - threaten to ruin everything. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Herb run into Lyndsey at yoga class and Herb is tempted to break the 'bro-code,' while Walden and Laurel try to spend the night together. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A selfish Peter loses his festive spirit and is visited by the ghosts of Christmas past, present and future, who take him on a journey around Quahog

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is asked to fill in for a mall Santa, but soon gets drunk on power when he realises that he can get away with anything while dressed as Father Christmas. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian crash holiday office parties for free food, drama and women

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian falls for a tech support girl in India and sets out to find her, with Stewie in tow. Meanwhile, Peter steals the show at Joe's bingo night

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire lands free plane tickets for a guys' trip to San Francisco, but his friends begin to doubt his flying abilities after getting a look at the autopilot function

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Professional American football player Rob Gronkowski moves into the neighbourhood, while Stewie uses steroids to increase his new bees' honey output, but it makes them aggressive

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan returns home from another dangerous mission to find Francine and the family have decorated the house for Christmas. Deciding their efforts do not live up to his high standards, he sets out to find the perfect tree, but meets an untimely end during his search, and battles the forces of evil to save his loved ones and earn another chance at life

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan finds himself a wanted man hunted by Roger the bounty hunter, and ends up in a town in need of a good guy to help Principal Lewis, Francine, Steve and Hayley

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Eager to take his team to the high-school baseball state championship, Cleveland persuades Holt to pose as a transfer student in place of his injured star pitcher. However, realising the bad example he has set Junior, the coach tries to clear his conscience

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland Jr and Kendra petition for a bill granting equal rights to overweight people, but the move is rejected and they flee to the more tolerant surroundings of Wisconsin, where they feel accepted

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is upset when he discovers Lyndsey failed to tell him that she broke up with Larry. Meanwhile, Walden endeavours to prove his masculinity after it is questioned. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jenny worries that she and Brooke aren't as close as they used to be and makes a plan to remedy that. When Lyndsey rejects Alan's ultimatum, he drowns his sorrows at his ex-wife Judith's house. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Return of the award-winning American comedy. In the first episode, Walden decides to reprioritise his life following a health scare on Halloween. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by team captain Stacey Solomon, who leads three friends in a series of games against Baga Chipz, Tom Read Wilson, Ivo Graham and Demi Sims

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Yasmeen's fate hangs in the balance as the trial draws to a close, the residents band together to halt Ray's bulldozer, and Daniel uncovers the truth about Adam and Carla

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Rishi has an accident, Meena has a plan, and Paul is uneasy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Gabriel joins Ruby on an outreach programme to a tea plantation, but discovers that she has not been completely honest about her reasons for wanting to go there. Back at the clinic, Lydia tries to help a patient who she believes is a victim of domestic abuse

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby is still reeling from her discoveries at the tea plantation, and must decide whether she can forgive past mistakes. Meanwhile, Mari goes head to head with Lydia when a surrogate mother gives birth in the hospital. Medical drama about a junior doctor who starts a new life in a South Indian hospital run by an eccentric English ex-pat. Starring Amrita Acharia, Nimmi Harasgama, Amanda Redman, James Krishna Floyd, Neil Morrissey, Darshan Jariwalla, Philip Jackson, Achint Kaur and Mandeep Dhillon

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. Lewis and Hathaway discover he had been imprisoned for trying to kill well-known atheist Tom Rattan and had only recently been released. The inspector soon has reason to believe the Rattan family is hiding something from him - but nothing prepares him for the evidence Dr Hobson uncovers at the post-mortem. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star, with guest appearances by Jenny Seagrove and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the North Yorkshire coastline as he investigates the nation's passion for being beside the sea, beginning in the historic town of Scarborough and travelling to the port of Whitby - where he goes on a ghostly adventure

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Rishi has an accident, Meena has a plan, and Paul is uneasy

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen thinks life with Bill is too boring. Martin hears that Don has been sent to a psychiatric hospital. Emily is suspicious to hear that Alec is behind the hospital raffle

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin and Natalie agree to stop seeing each other. Mike is frustrated that Don will not stand trial. Alan suggests to Fiona that she should move in with him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will threatens Zoe and warns her to keep away from his mum. Kelly wants nothing more to do with Chris. Eric blames Will for the cellar flooding at the wine bar

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy and Mandy have a row about money. Eric is suspicious when he spots Dee meeting up with Will. Zoe is shocked when Becky tells her she is falling in love with her

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Reverend Jacob Thwaite returns home to find his wife has been murdered, but while Rachel is suspicious of his alibi it's a psychic convention in the town that seems to hold the key to the case. Meanwhile, Peggy devises a plan to sell trout to a hotel in Scarborough that lands her and David in trouble with the authorities. Trudie Goodwin guests

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As the team see victory in their grasp, Fred has a heart attack. Cricketing drama, starring Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: With storm clouds gathering, the islanders realise they can no longer ignore the rumblings of war, just as it looks as though the Durrells - particularly Louisa and Spiros - may have found their happy ending. Larry marks his return by throwing an audacious play for the locals, but there are surprises for every member of the family. The fourth season of the light-hearted drama reaches its conclusion. Starring Keeley Hawes, Josh O'Connor, Milo Parker, Daisy Waterstone, Callum Woodhouse and Alexis Georgoulis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff and Phil find two men acting suspiciously, but two others escape from the scene and the officers soon find themselves in danger and under siege. At the Aidensfield Arms the residents gather to watch the moon landing, although one guest gives Gina cause for concern

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Becky tells Zoe that Tony knows about them. Eric thinks Dee is planning to leave him. Kathy and Rachel are asked out on a date by two firemen

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Becky acts coldly towards Zoe. Eric accuses Dee of kissing Will. The police locate Dee with news from the Philippines that her mother has died

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen is disappointed with her night out with Bill. Fiona is pleased that Alan is going to move in with her. The raffle at the Rovers results in tragedy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is uncomfortable being described as a miracle worker. Natalie invites Kevin to spend the night with her. Conway tells Jack how Alec ripped him off

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At a Christmas family get-together, a cracker riddle states two people will die before midnight on Boxing Day, and just a few hours later family matriarch Lydia meets an untimely end - giving Barnaby the perfect reason to escape a boring Christmas with his in-laws. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: Period drama, set in 18th-century Delhi, years before the territory fell under British colonial rule. John Beecham (Tom Bateman), a former soldier for the East India Company, buys a mansion in the area in the hope of starting a new life. His shadowy past is soon the talk of the household, particularly since he arrives accompanied by a mysterious child of unknown parentage

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: University lecturer Dr Leah Dale has always prided herself on her academic integrity, so when final-year student Rose Vaughan submits a suspiciously top-grade essay, Leah is quick to call her out. But there's more going on than meets the eye as Rose takes the challenge as a personal affront, and what begins as a seemingly open-and-shut case of academic deception soon spirals out of control. Drama, starring Katherine Kelly and Molly Windsor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when a few unlikely liaisons are formed at the Brent Park barbecue. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa worries Dr Timoney's suggestion that she and Martin aren't meant to be together might have prompted him to leave Portwenn for good. Calling on Ruth, Morwenna and Janice for help, she tries to track the down the missing GP, with no luck. However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Peggy hatches a plan to save Bernie when he becomes the target of a predatory widow (guest star Mel Martin). Carol struggles to control her feelings of guilt when a newborn baby dies in her care, and grows more distressed when Rob arrests the bereaved father after a violent altercation at the Aidensfield Arms. Thomas Craig also guests

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dee refuses to speak to Eric and books a flight home for her mother's funeral, so he dashes to the airport to stop her from leaving. Will and Marlon fight in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric decides to close the wine bar. Paddy panics when his mum announces that she is coming to visit. Emma hears about what happened between Becky and Zoe

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is concerned to find Maureen crying in the shop. Rita warns Natalie to keep away from Kevin. Samantha lords it over Judy at the Rovers

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly spends an uncomfortable night in the shop's backroom. Samantha worries that she gives men the wrong impression. Deirdre takes over at Sunliners

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate a murder committed on the first night of a quiz weekend held at Chaucer College. Sealing the college grounds to interrogate the remaining 11 contestants, the pair discover the victim had a track record for sexual harassment involving fellow competitors, as well as past dealings with a shady engineering business funded by Russian gangsters and masterminded by Oxford professor Charles Milner - another quiz fan. With guest stars Alan Davies (QI), Timothy West (Bleak House) and Nicholas Farrell (The Iron Lady)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a time of joy and happiness in Midsomer for once - Christmas is coming, and DCI Barnaby and his wife Sarah are soon to become parents. But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. Local historian Brendan Pearce thinks blacksmith's daughter Rose Wilton is seeking revenge from beyond the grave, but Barnaby and sidekick Charlie Nelson try to find a more earthly motive behind the murder. Starring Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee, with Fiona Dolman, Les Dennis, Elizabeth Berrington, Mark Heap, Emily Joyce, James Murray and Hannah Tointon

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: With the arrival of his mother Henrietta from London and the discovery of his younger brother Daniel in a military camp close by, John has finally fulfilled his dream of having his family reunited. But, the arrival of the mysterious Chandrika throws the household into chaos and threatens to break the family apart

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: With relations becoming increasingly hostile, Leah resorts to more extreme measures to expose Rose, and as tensions flare between Leah and Adam, could it be that her husband is taking Rose's side? Later, Rose seems to pursue a more personal retaliation against Leah, but who is telling the truth? Katherine Kelly, Molly Windsor and Tom Goodman-Hill star

Meanwhile, Peggy devises a plan to sell trout to a hotel in Scarborough that lands her and David in trouble with the authorities. Trudie Goodwin guests

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As the team see victory in their grasp, Fred has a heart attack. Cricketing drama, starring Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: With storm clouds gathering, the islanders realise they can no longer ignore the rumblings of war, just as it looks as though the Durrells - particularly Louisa and Spiros - may have found their happy ending. Larry marks his return by throwing an audacious play for the locals, but there are surprises for every member of the family. The fourth season of the light-hearted drama reaches its conclusion. Starring Keeley Hawes, Josh O'Connor, Milo Parker, Daisy Waterstone, Callum Woodhouse and Alexis Georgoulis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff and Phil find two men acting suspiciously, but two others escape from the scene and the officers soon find themselves in danger and under siege. At the Aidensfield Arms the residents gather to watch the moon landing, although one guest gives Gina cause for concern

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Becky tells Zoe that Tony knows about them. Eric thinks Dee is planning to leave him. Kathy and Rachel are asked out on a date by two firemen

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Becky acts coldly towards Zoe. Eric accuses Dee of kissing Will. The police locate Dee with news from the Philippines that her mother has died

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen is disappointed with her night out with Bill. Fiona is pleased that Alan is going to move in with her. The raffle at the Rovers results in tragedy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is uncomfortable being described as a miracle worker. Natalie invites Kevin to spend the night with her. Conway tells Jack how Alec ripped him off

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At a Christmas family get-together, a cracker riddle states two people will die before midnight on Boxing Day, and just a few hours later family matriarch Lydia meets an untimely end - giving Barnaby the perfect reason to escape a boring Christmas with his in-laws. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: Period drama, set in 18th-century Delhi, years before the territory fell under British colonial rule. John Beecham (Tom Bateman), a former soldier for the East India Company, buys a mansion in the area in the hope of starting a new life. His shadowy past is soon the talk of the household, particularly since he arrives accompanied by a mysterious child of unknown parentage

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: University lecturer Dr Leah Dale has always prided herself on her academic integrity, so when final-year student Rose Vaughan submits a suspiciously top-grade essay, Leah is quick to call her out. But there's more going on than meets the eye as Rose takes the challenge as a personal affront, and what begins as a seemingly open-and-shut case of academic deception soon spirals out of control. Drama, starring Katherine Kelly and Molly Windsor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when a few unlikely liaisons are formed at the Brent Park barbecue. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa worries Dr Timoney's suggestion that she and Martin aren't meant to be together might have prompted him to leave Portwenn for good. Calling on Ruth, Morwenna and Janice for help, she tries to track the down the missing GP, with no luck. However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Peggy hatches a plan to save Bernie when he becomes the target of a predatory widow (guest star Mel Martin). Carol struggles to control her feelings of guilt when a newborn baby dies in her care, and grows more distressed when Rob arrests the bereaved father after a violent altercation at the Aidensfield Arms. Thomas Craig also guests

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dee refuses to speak to Eric and books a flight home for her mother's funeral, so he dashes to the airport to stop her from leaving. Will and Marlon fight in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric decides to close the wine bar. Paddy panics when his mum announces that she is coming to visit. Emma hears about what happened between Becky and Zoe

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is concerned to find Maureen crying in the shop. Rita warns Natalie to keep away from Kevin. Samantha lords it over Judy at the Rovers

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly spends an uncomfortable night in the shop's backroom. Samantha worries that she gives men the wrong impression. Deirdre takes over at Sunliners

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate a murder committed on the first night of a quiz weekend held at Chaucer College. Sealing the college grounds to interrogate the remaining 11 contestants, the pair discover the victim had a track record for sexual harassment involving fellow competitors, as well as past dealings with a shady engineering business funded by Russian gangsters and masterminded by Oxford professor Charles Milner - another quiz fan. With guest stars Alan Davies (QI), Timothy West (Bleak House) and Nicholas Farrell (The Iron Lady)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a time of joy and happiness in Midsomer for once - Christmas is coming, and DCI Barnaby and his wife Sarah are soon to become parents. But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. Local historian Brendan Pearce thinks blacksmith's daughter Rose Wilton is seeking revenge from beyond the grave, but Barnaby and sidekick Charlie Nelson try to find a more earthly motive behind the murder. Starring Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee, with Fiona Dolman, Les Dennis, Elizabeth Berrington, Mark Heap, Emily Joyce, James Murray and Hannah Tointon

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: With the arrival of his mother Henrietta from London and the discovery of his younger brother Daniel in a military camp close by, John has finally fulfilled his dream of having his family reunited. But, the arrival of the mysterious Chandrika throws the household into chaos and threatens to break the family apart

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: With relations becoming increasingly hostile, Leah resorts to more extreme measures to expose Rose, and as tensions flare between Leah and Adam, could it be that her husband is taking Rose's side? Later, Rose seems to pursue a more personal retaliation against Leah, but who is telling the truth? Katherine Kelly, Molly Windsor and Tom Goodman-Hill star

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Hornblower's resourcefulness turns the attack on the Spanish-held fortress into a famous victory, but Buckland's treachery puts his life into further danger. Surviving a risky mission to blow up the fortifications and an attempt by the prisoners to take command of the Renown, the hero faces the death penalty for pushing Captain Sawyer into the hold - and hopes Archie's dying words can save him from the noose. Naval adventure, based on the stories by CS Forester, and starring Ioan Gruffudd

programme.title: The Cowboys (1972)
programme.desc: An ageing rancher finds himself short-handed when his workers abandon him during a gold rush, and grudgingly hires a group of schoolboys to help him on a 400-mile cattle drive. Although at first they feel out of their depth, the youngsters quickly learn to work like men - but face a dangerous challenge ahead as a gang of rustlers begins to stalk them. Western, with John Wayne, Roscoe Lee Browne and Bruce Dern

Light-hearted crime drama, guest starring Pete Postlethwaite, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The team assists the Russian authorities in tracking down a former spy, whose defection threatens international security. Cowley is anxious to locate him first amid fears others may prefer to silence him permanently. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar has to find out whether a cool blonde is a secret agent or a crook - and if she could do him harm. Sixties spy adventure, directed by and starring Roger Moore

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo investigate the killings of a drug dealer and an SI-10 officer and are soon on the trail of an assassin hired by a powerful American crimelord. Drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is held by the police after a car he is importing from Europe is impounded by drugs officers, so Terry goes in search of those he suspects are responsible for his boss' predicament - the vehicle's original owners. programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the opening day of the tournament from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Reigning champion Neil Robertson will be hoping to defend his title as he goes up against the top 32 money earners on the one-year list. Tonight, there's first-round action as players compete in best-of-seven-frame matches for a place in the last 16. Robertson was made to work hard in his opener 12 months ago, eventually defeating Michael Holt 4-1, and Mark Selby suffered a shock 4-3 defeat at the hands of Xiao Guodong. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the opening day of the tournament from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Reigning champion Neil Robertson will be hoping to defend his title as he goes up against the top 32 money earners on the one-year list. Tonight, there's first-round action as players compete in best-of-seven-frame matches for a place in the last 16. Robertson was made to work hard in his opener 12 months ago, eventually defeating Michael Holt 4-1, and Mark Selby suffered a shock 4-3 defeat at the hands of Xiao Guodong. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Jane sets aside her doubts to offer Nicolette support, Susan notices something between Audrey and Toadie, and Karl finds out the identity of the anonymous donor
